meta.name: Operation BALTIC SENTINEL
meta.description: Notional NATO multinational corps operation on the eastern flank across 96 hours. Demonstrates phase-aware criticality with a logistics twist: the Phase I rail-corridor cut and coastal-LOC degradation converge all corps sustainment onto the single Gdynia port terminal in Phase II.
assumptions: All units, systems, locations, links, events, and timings in this scenario are entirely fictional and were generated solely for product demonstration.
Real basing and geographic names are used only to make the map read plausibly; no real-world force disposition, capability, or plan is represented.
H-hours are exercise-relative: H+0 is scenario start, not any real-world date or event.

Scenario themes drawn from unclassified open-source analysis of Baltic and eastern-flank logistics and infrastructure (European rail gauge break, port throughput limits, the Suwalki corridor, undersea cable incidents, GPS jamming reports); all units, systems, and events are fictional.

phases.label: Set Conditions
phases.notes: Shaping and prepo draw; sustainment flows uncontested on two ground LOCs and the Gdynia seaport; enclave jamming and subsea incidents probe the flank.

phases.label: Close Operations
phases.main_effort: MN Corps NORTHEAST deep fires — HIMARS counter-fire vs OPFOR SRBM and enclave IADS
phases.notes: Corridor cut — everything moves through Gdynia; GPS jamming degrades precision logistics and the coastal LOC.

phases.label: Consolidate
phases.notes: Rail bridges and the Baltic trunk under repair; throughput disperses off the single terminal; risk shifts to interceptor stocks and port endurance.

nodes.name: MN Corps NORTHEAST Main Command Post
nodes.mission: Provides multinational corps mission command and synchronizes eastern-flank operations across all warfighting functions for Operation BALTIC SENTINEL.
nodes.vulnerability_notes: Main CP, COP servers, and the SATCOM gateway draw on the same host-nation power grid segment.

nodes.name: MN Division NORTH Headquarters
nodes.mission: Commands the forward multinational division defending the corridor approaches.

nodes.name: MN Division NORTH Tactical Command Post
nodes.mission: Forward mission command node controlling the close fight in the Suwalki corridor during Phase II.

nodes.name: Corps Fires Cell
nodes.mission: Plans and controls corps deep fires and manages the HIMARS counter-fire kill chain.

nodes.name: 87th Corps Sustainment Brigade HQ
nodes.mission: Directs corps sustainment through the Gdynia seaport, the Powidz aerial port, and the ground LOC network.

nodes.name: 1-77 Field Artillery (HIMARS)
nodes.mission: Delivers long-range precision rocket fires against OPFOR SRBM launchers and enclave air defense radars.
nodes.vulnerability_notes: Rocket resupply single-threads through the Gdynia terminal once the rail corridor is cut at H+36.

nodes.name: 2-43 ADA Battalion (GBAD)
nodes.mission: Provides ground-based air defense of the Gdynia port complex and the corps Main CP.
nodes.vulnerability_notes: Interceptor reloads arrive by sea; magazine depth is measured in days under sustained SRBM salvos.

nodes.name: Corps EW Company
nodes.mission: Conducts electronic attack against enclave jamming and early-warning emitters.

nodes.name: Corps Cyber Effects Planning Cell
nodes.mission: Plans and coordinates offensive cyber effects against enclave IADS command and control.

nodes.name: SOF Reconnaissance Detachment NORTH
nodes.mission: Conducts special reconnaissance along the corridor flank, cueing counter-fire against OPFOR SRBM movement.

nodes.name: NATO AEW&C Orbit BALTIC GUARDIAN
nodes.mission: Airborne early warning and control orbit over central Poland feeding the recognized air picture to corps air defense.
nodes.vulnerability_notes: Enclave long-range SAM coverage pushes the orbit west in Phase II, thinning radar coverage over the corridor.

nodes.name: Seaport of Debarkation GDYNIA
nodes.mission: Receives strategic sealift and prepositioned-stock draw at the Gdynia quays for discharge into the port logistics terminal.
nodes.vulnerability_notes: Deep-draft berths and heavy cranes are concentrated on two quays — the port-throughput chokepoint open-source Baltic logistics studies flag.

nodes.name: Port Logistics Terminal GDYNIA
nodes.mission: Stages, marshals, and pushes all seaborne sustainment from the Gdynia quays onto the corps distribution network.
nodes.vulnerability_notes: Becomes the sole feed for division sustainment, ammunition, and interceptor reloads once the rail corridor is cut (H+36) and the coastal LOC degrades under GPS jamming — a Phase II single point of failure that does not exist in Phase I.

nodes.name: Airfield Logistics Node POWIDZ (APOD)
nodes.mission: Operates the aerial port of debarkation receiving the Ramstein airlift shuttle for time-sensitive corps cargo.
nodes.vulnerability_notes: Single large ramp and fuel farm — the airfield-concentration problem that makes APODs the first SRBM aimpoints.
nodes.notes: Ramp and fuel hydrants damaged by the Phase II SRBM salvo; partial throughput until repairs complete.

nodes.name: Rail Gauge-Break Yard MOCKAVA
nodes.mission: Transloads corps sustainment between European standard-gauge and broad-gauge rolling stock at the Mockava/Sestokai break-of-gauge yard.
nodes.vulnerability_notes: Fixed cranes and limited transload sidings cap corridor rail throughput — the gauge-break bottleneck every open-source Baltic mobility study flags.
nodes.notes: Approach bridges struck at H+36; yard offline through Phase II, partial capability restored by engineer repair in Phase III.

nodes.name: Coastal Road-Rail Corridor SIAULIAI
nodes.mission: Coastal road and rail line of communication carrying convoy and rail traffic from the Polish ports north toward the division rear.
nodes.vulnerability_notes: Convoy navigation and movement-tracking on the corridor depend on GPS; persistent enclave jamming plus interdiction strikes cut effective throughput in half after H+36.
nodes.notes: Degraded from H+36 by GPS jamming and precision strikes on chokepoints; residual throughput only in Phases II-III.

nodes.name: Prepositioned Stocks Site ODER
nodes.mission: Issues prepositioned combat and sustainment stocks for onward movement to the corps.
nodes.vulnerability_notes: Draw rate is gated by the LOCs east: rail through Mockava in Phase I, coastal sealift into Gdynia after the corridor is cut.

nodes.name: Ammunition Transfer Point SUWALKI
nodes.mission: Forward ammunition transfer point issuing Class V to corps fires units in the corridor.

nodes.name: MN Division NORTH Sustainment Battalion
nodes.mission: Distributes all commodity classes from the corps LOCs to division units in the corridor.
nodes.vulnerability_notes: Fed by two ground LOCs plus the port in Phase I; single-threaded through the Gdynia terminal after H+36.

nodes.name: 52nd Engineer Battalion (Rail Repair)
nodes.mission: Restores rail bridges and the gauge-break yard during consolidation.

nodes.name: Corps SATCOM Gateway BALTIC
nodes.mission: Aggregates corps wideband SATCOM reach-back for mission command, fires, intelligence, and sustainment traffic.
nodes.vulnerability_notes: Mission command, fires, and sustainment data all concentrate on this single gateway path; enclave uplink jamming squeezes it from Phase I.

nodes.name: NATO SATCOM IV-A (Wideband)
nodes.mission: Provides primary wideband SATCOM transport between the corps gateway and the Nordic ground station.

nodes.name: Commercial Ka-band GEO BALTICSAT-2
nodes.mission: Leased commercial Ka-band transponder providing alternate wideband transport for corps reach-back.

nodes.name: Ground Station NORDIC
nodes.mission: Regional wideband teleport in southern Norway that terminates corps SATCOM reach-back into the theater network.
nodes.vulnerability_notes: All corps wideband reach-back lands at this single teleport — a standing comms concentration in every phase.

nodes.name: Undersea Cable Landing KOLOBRZEG
nodes.mission: Baltic undersea fiber trunk landing carrying bulk corps data traffic to the theater network.
nodes.vulnerability_notes: Baltic subsea infrastructure incidents 2023-2025 are the open-source theme: a single trunk with no diverse path and hostile subsurface activity around it.
nodes.notes: Trunk damaged by subsurface action at H+40; cable-ship repair begins in Phase III.

nodes.name: Theater Network Node RAMSTEIN
nodes.mission: Theater network point of presence connecting corps reach-back into strategic transport.

nodes.name: Mission Command Platform (Corps COP)
nodes.mission: Hosts the corps common operational picture and mission command services for all command posts.

nodes.name: Logistics Data System
nodes.mission: Aggregates supply, movement, and port-throughput data feeding the sustainment and corps common operating pictures.
nodes.vulnerability_notes: Precision-logistics scheduling assumes GPS-disciplined timing and tracking; enclave jamming forces fallback to manual batch reporting.

nodes.name: Sustainment C2 System
nodes.mission: Logistics common operating picture tracking stocks, movements, and commodity status for the corps.

nodes.name: Corps Intelligence Database
nodes.mission: Stores fused all-source intelligence and target materials serving fires, the COP, and the division.

nodes.name: Fires Data System
nodes.mission: Processes fire missions and technical fire direction for the HIMARS battalion.
nodes.vulnerability_notes: Depends on GPS-derived timing; enclave jamming slows Phase II counter-fire processing.

nodes.name: OPFOR Enclave IADS Complex
nodes.mission: Layered surface-to-air missile and early-warning radar complex covering the enclave and the southern Baltic.

geo.location_name: Notional OPFOR enclave air defense complex

nodes.name: OPFOR SRBM Brigade
nodes.mission: Delivers short-range ballistic missile salvos against airfields, ports, and corridor chokepoints.

nodes.name: OPFOR Baltic Surface Action Group
nodes.mission: Surface action group threatening sea approaches to the Polish ports and loitering near subsea infrastructure.

nodes.name: OPFOR EW/Jamming Regiment
nodes.mission: Conducts GPS and SATCOM uplink jamming from the enclave across the corridor and southern Baltic.

links.notes: Corps command relationship with the forward multinational division.
Division TAC assumes control of the corridor close fight in Phase II.
SOF detachment reach-back rides narrowband SATCOM, independent of the corps gateway.
COP server stack is hosted in the Main CP on shared host-nation power.
Primary reach-back transport for the corps common operational picture in all phases.
CUI logistics status feed crosses a classification boundary into the SECRET COP through a one-way guard.
Fire mission and technical fire direction data to the HIMARS battalion.
Target intelligence and battle damage assessment feed the counter-fire chain.
Special reconnaissance reporting on OPFOR SRBM movement along the corridor flank.
Recognized air picture and track data from the AEW&C orbit.
Early-warning cueing for port and CP air defense — orbit displacement in Phase II lengthens warning time.
Legacy HF fallback net awaiting multinational crypto fill — currently passes SECRET traffic unencrypted.
Primary wideband uplink.
Leased commercial Ka-band alternate uplink.
All corps wideband traffic lands at the NORDIC teleport — a standing comms concentration in every phase.
Teleport-to-theater terrestrial segment — no diverse terrestrial route north of Oslo.
Bulk COP and intelligence replication rides the Baltic trunk; degraded after the H+40 subsea incident.
Single Baltic undersea trunk with no diverse path — the 2023-2025 subsea-incident theme; damaged at H+40.
Sustainment C2 reach-back rides the corps gateway — logistics data joins the comms concentration.
Port throughput and vessel discharge status feed.
Gauge-break transload status hand-carried daily to the sustainment COP — manual reporting gate with roughly 12-hour latency.
Prepo draw moves east by standard-gauge rail to the Mockava transload; corridor bridges are struck and the line cut at H+36.
Broad-gauge onward movement to the division rear; lost when the corridor is interdicted at H+36.
Class V by rail to the Suwalki ATP; lost when the corridor is interdicted at H+36.
Onward movement north over the coastal road-rail LOC; residual throughput only after H+36 GPS jamming and strikes.
Coastal LOC convoy and rail deliveries into the division rear — GPS-dependent movement tracking.
Everything discharged at the Gdynia quays crosses the single logistics terminal.
Rail corridor cut — prepo draw diverted to coastal sealift into Gdynia.
Corridor cut at H+36 — division sustainment single-threaded through the Gdynia terminal.
Ammunition throughput rerouted from the rail corridor through Gdynia after H+36.
Interceptor reloads arrive by sea and issue through the terminal — magazine depth rides on Gdynia.
Forward staging packages for the division TAC move through the terminal once the corridor is cut.
Class V rocket pods to the HIMARS battalion.
Division distribution to the TAC and corridor units.
Time-sensitive Class VIII/IX by the Ramstein-Powidz airlift shuttle; ramp damage halves throughput in Phase II.
GBAD coverage of the Gdynia complex — corps defended asset list serial 1 from H+36.
A single battalion stretches to cover both the port complex and the Powidz ramp.
Suppression of enclave air defense radars to open corridors for AEW&C and airlift.
Counter-fire tasking: hold the SRBM brigade at risk to thin follow-on salvos.
Counter-jamming electronic attack against enclave GPS and uplink jammers.
Offensive cyber effects against enclave IADS command and control, authorized H+8 to H+60.
Special reconnaissance maintains track custody of SRBM launcher movement.
SRBM salvo craters the Powidz ramp at H+38 — the airfield-concentration aimpoint.
The terminal everything now moves through sits on the SRBM target deck — the Phase II convergence OPFOR is built to exploit.
Interdiction strikes on the corridor approach bridges cut the gauge-break line at H+36.
Uplink jamming from the enclave squeezes the corps wideband path.
Persistent GPS jamming over the corridor degrades convoy navigation and precision-logistics tracking.
The SAG threatens the sea approaches to Gdynia, forcing escorted convoys and slowing discharge.
Subsurface activity loiters near the Baltic trunk — the cable-incident pattern of 2023-2025.
Long-range SAM coverage from the enclave pushes the AEW&C orbit west of the Vistula.
Enclave IADS umbrella covers the SRBM garrisons.
Bridge repair and yard restoration during consolidation returns the rail LOC to partial service.

vulnerabilities.title: Rail gauge-break transload capacity at Mockava
vulnerabilities.operational_impact: Every railcar moving east changes gauge at one yard with fixed cranes and limited sidings. The transload rate caps corridor rail throughput well below demand, and a single interdiction of the approach bridges — as happens at H+36 — removes the entire rail LOC rather than degrading it.
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Pre-stage bridging and transload equipment with the engineer battalion, rehearse yard bypass using road transload sites, and contract additional gauge-changeover bogies before Phase II.
vulnerabilities.notes: Analyst-asserted from open-source Baltic mobility studies; capacity is invisible to link topology.

vulnerabilities.title: Berth and crane concentration at the Gdynia terminal
vulnerabilities.operational_impact: Deep-draft berths and heavy cranes concentrate on two quays feeding one logistics terminal. After H+36 that terminal carries division sustainment, ammunition, and interceptor reloads simultaneously; a single successful SRBM strike or mining of the approaches halts every commodity class at once.
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Nominate the terminal to the defended asset list, disperse discharge across Gdansk and Swinoujscie lighterage sites, and pre-position port repair and salvage assets before Phase II.
vulnerabilities.notes: Analyst-asserted; complements the topology-derived Phase II single-point finding on the terminal.

vulnerabilities.title: GPS dependence of precision logistics
vulnerabilities.operational_impact: Convoy navigation, movement tracking, and delivery scheduling all assume GPS-disciplined position and timing. Enclave jamming reported across the corridor from H+18 forces fallback to paper strip maps and batch reporting, roughly halving effective coastal-LOC throughput exactly when it becomes the only ground route.
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Issue inertial and map-based navigation fallbacks to convoy leads, rehearse jamming-degraded convoy battle drills, and shift the logistics data systems to holdover clocks with courier reconciliation.
vulnerabilities.notes: Analyst-asserted from open-source Baltic GPS-interference reporting.

vulnerabilities.title: GBAD interceptor magazine depth
vulnerabilities.operational_impact: Sustained SRBM salvo exchanges deplete GBAD interceptor stocks in days. Reloads arrive by sea through the same Gdynia terminal the battalion defends — a circular dependency: losing the port empties the magazine, and an empty magazine loses the port.
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Enforce defended-asset-list engagement discipline, pre-stage reloads forward of the port before Phase II, and rehearse shoot-look-shoot procedures to stretch the magazine.
vulnerabilities.notes: Analyst-asserted; magazine depth is invisible to link topology.

activities.name: Prepo draw and forward staging
activities.note: Prepositioned stocks issue at the Oder site and move east by rail through the Mockava gauge break while the corridor is still open.

activities.name: Counter-SOF sweep — corps rear area
activities.note: Rear-area sweep against OPFOR sabotage teams reconnoitering LOC bridges and the port approaches.

activities.name: Cyber effects vs enclave IADS C2
activities.note: Offensive cyber effects degrade enclave air defense command and control ahead of and during close operations.

activities.name: GPS jamming from the enclave
activities.note: Persistent GPS jamming across the corridor — the open-source Baltic jamming-report pattern — degrades convoy navigation and precision-logistics tracking.

activities.name: Rail corridor interdiction
activities.note: Precision strikes drop the corridor approach bridges at H+36; the gauge-break line is cut and the corridor contested.

activities.name: OPFOR SRBM salvo vs APOD POWIDZ
activities.note: SRBM salvo craters the Powidz ramp and fuel farm; the airlift shuttle drops to partial throughput.

activities.name: Undersea cable disruption — Baltic
activities.note: Subsurface action damages the Baltic trunk off Kolobrzeg — mirroring the 2023-2025 subsea-infrastructure incidents.

activities.name: Convoy surge via coastal LOC
activities.note: Escorted convoys push everything the degraded coastal corridor can still carry while the rail line is down.

activities.name: Port throughput surge — GDYNIA
activities.note: Round-the-clock discharge at the Gdynia quays — the single terminal now carries division sustainment, ammunition, and interceptor reloads at once.

activities.name: AEW&C orbit displacement west
activities.note: Enclave long-range SAM coverage pushes the orbit west of the Vistula, thinning radar coverage over the corridor.

activities.name: HIMARS counter-fire vs SRBM brigade
activities.note: Corps main effort counter-fire, cued by SOF track custody, thins the follow-on salvos aimed at the port.

activities.name: Counter-jamming electronic attack
activities.note: Electronic attack windows against enclave jammers buy convoy navigation windows on the coastal LOC.

activities.name: Rail bridge repair and yard restoration
activities.note: Engineer bridging and yard repair return the gauge-break line to partial service, taking pressure off the Gdynia terminal.

activities.name: Cable-ship repair operation
activities.note: Escorted cable-ship repair of the Baltic trunk; restoration expected by end of Phase III.

notes.author: G5 Plans (synthetic)
notes.text: DECISION: Nominate the Port Logistics Terminal GDYNIA to the corps defended asset list NLT H+36. Once the rail corridor is cut and the coastal LOC degrades under GPS jamming, the terminal is the sole feed for division sustainment, ammunition, and interceptor reloads — a single point of failure that does not exist in Phase I.

notes.author: G4 (synthetic)
notes.text: DECISION: Pre-stage the 52nd Engineer Battalion with bridging and transload equipment vicinity Elk NLT H+30, so gauge-break repair can begin the moment the corridor is retaken — every day the Mockava yard stays down is a day everything rides one terminal.

notes.author: G5 Plans (synthetic)
notes.text: DECISION: Disperse APOD throughput off the single Powidz ramp before Phase II — pre-negotiate secondary strips, stage rapid runway repair kits, and split the Ramstein shuttle across two fields so one SRBM salvo cannot stop the air line of communication.
